news.headline: Novo Nordisk's denecimig (Mim8) demonstrated positive long-term safety and efficacy in phase 3 hemophilia A FRONTIER extension study at ISTH 2026
news.summary: Novo reported durable long-term safety and efficacy for its once-monthly hemophilia A antibody denecimig (Mim8) in the Phase 3 FRONTIER extension study, presented at ISTH 2026. The data supports pipeline diversification beyond the GLP-1 franchise into rare bleeding disorders.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk A/S - share repurchase programme
news.summary: As of 3 July 2026 Novo had bought back 23,009,179 B shares at an average price of DKK 270.32 under the up-to-DKK-15bn, 12-month buyback launched 4 February 2026. The steady repurchase pace signals management confidence and provides ongoing capital return support to the stock.

news.headline: Medicare obesity drug GLP-1 coverage starting July 1
news.summary: Eligible Medicare beneficiaries gained access to Wegovy (injection and pill) for a $50/month copay from July 1, 2026 via the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program, running through end-2027. Novo and Eli Lilly agreed to supply the drugs to CMS at a net price of $245/month, opening a large new reimbursed patient pool ahead of the January 2027 list-price cut.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk (NVO) Upgraded to Buy at Nordea
news.summary: Nordea upgraded Novo Nordisk to Buy from Hold with a DKK 350 price target, citing favorable near-term news flow and expectations that Wegovy pill sales could beat market expectations. The upgrade came days after the UK approval of the Wegovy pill lifted sentiment.

news.headline: CagriSema Diabetes Data Boost Before FDA Decision
news.summary: New CagriSema diabetes data presented at the ADA 2026 Scientific Sessions (New Orleans, June 5-8) reinforced the GLP-1/amylin combination's profile, with weight loss of up to ~23% in trials. The readout strengthens Novo's case ahead of an expected Q4 2026 FDA decision on its next-generation obesity drug.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk: Wegovy pill (semaglutide tablets) becomes first daily GLP-1 weight-loss pill approved in the UK
news.summary: The UK's MHRA approved the Wegovy pill for weight management, making the UK the first European market and third globally (after the US and UAE) to clear the oral GLP-1. Novo expects the pill to become available via private prescription within weeks, extending its oral-obesity franchise ahead of Eli Lilly's international rollout.

catalysts.label: H1/Q2 2026 earnings release
catalysts.detail: Financial report for the six months to 30 June 2026, due 5 August 2026 (07:30 CEST); focus on Wegovy pill adoption post-UK launch and Medicare Bridge uptake, underlying obesity-care momentum ex one-off pricing, and any guidance revision.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on CagriSema
catalysts.detail: Expected FDA verdict on the first once-weekly GLP-1/amylin combination (semaglutide + cagrilintide) for chronic weight management, filed on REDEFINE 1/2 data; trials showed up to ~20-23% weight loss. Key re-rating catalyst.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on oral semaglutide 25 mg for obesity
catalysts.detail: Supplemental application for higher-dose oral semaglutide 25 mg in obesity under FDA review, with a decision expected by end of 2026 to broaden the oral GLP-1 franchise versus Lilly's orforglipron (Foundayo).

catalysts.label: Higher-dose CagriSema Phase 3 start
catalysts.detail: A higher-dose version of CagriSema is scheduled to enter Phase 3 in the second half of 2026; the larger REDEFINE-11 outcomes study reads out in H1 2027.

catalysts.label: U.S. list-price cut takes effect
catalysts.detail: Wegovy, Ozempic and Rybelsus list prices drop to ~$675/month (roughly -50% / -35%), coinciding with new Medicare-negotiated pricing under the GLP-1 Bridge; a margin headwind but a volume-defense move.

pulse: Novo Nordisk's newsflow has turned more constructive over the past six weeks: the Wegovy pill won UK approval (first in Europe) and gained U.S. Medicare Bridge reimbursement from 1 July at a $50 copay, while fresh CagriSema diabetes data and a Nordea upgrade to Buy have helped the stock claw back from its March lows. The company also continues an active DKK 15bn buyback and is diversifying its pipeline with promising hemophilia (denecimig) data. The key swing factor remains the Q4 2026 FDA decision on CagriSema for obesity, alongside the August 5 H1 earnings print, against a backdrop of intensifying competition from Eli Lilly's Foundayo pill and a scheduled ~50% U.S. list-price cut in January 2027.
